preview.summary: England, among the tournament favourites under Thomas Tuchel, face a Ghana side returning to the World Cup with a young, energetic core. The Three Lions boast elite attacking talent led by an in-form Harry Kane, while Ghana will rely on Mohammed Kudus and pace on the break. Expect England to press for control against a Black Stars side happy to counter.
preview.homeForm: England cruised through qualifying under Tuchel and arrive among the favourites with a deep, talented squad.
preview.awayForm: Ghana topped CAF Group I with eight wins from ten; ranked in the mid-70s by FIFA.
preview.homeKey: Harry Kane, captain fresh off 36 Bundesliga goals for Bayern.
preview.awayKey: Mohammed Kudus, West Ham forward and Ghana's qualifying talisman.
storylines: Tuchel's ruthless squad call leaving out Palmer and Foden
Kudus and Inaki Williams powering Ghana's counter
England chasing a statement group-stage win
preview.h2h: England and Ghana have met only in a 2011 friendly, which finished 1-1.
